---
title: "Test of bluetooth loggers - Testing mobile loggers"
author: "Aurore Ponchon"
date: "01/04/2022"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message=F)
```

## Experiment: identifying best mobile loggers

This experiment was run with 6 stationary loggers placed sufficiently far from each other to detect only mobile loggers in their vicinity.

```{r data import , message=F, include=F, echo=F}

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(lubridate)

#function to format logs and relate distance and time intervals
get_formatted_data<- function (#XLfile=xlfile,  #excel file linking distance and time intervals
                             Date=date.exp,    #date of experiment
                             Path.Data=data.path,  #path to the folder containing log data from stationary loggers
                          #   Position=position,  # table with position of stationary loggers
                             Test=test){   #test number

  
  #import table with time and distance recorded in the field
# dist<-read_excel(xlfile) %>%
#   mutate(Time.start = ymd_hms(paste(ymd(Date),format(Time.start, "%H:%M:%S"))),
#          Time.end= ymd_hms(paste(ymd(Date),format(Time.end, "%H:%M:%S"))),
#          Time.int=time_length(interval(Time.start,Time.end),"mins"))
# dist$Interval<-1:nrow(dist)
# 

#import actual data from loggers
files <- list.files(Path.Data, pattern = "*.csv") 

#name data and set right classes
logs <- files %>%
            map(~ read_csv(file.path(Path.Data,files),
                           col_names=F,
                           col_types=c('i','i','T','n'))) %>% 
            reduce(rbind) %>% 
            rename(ID=X1,
                   Mobile = X2,
                   Date=X3,
                   Signal=X4) %>% 
            filter(Mobile>100) %>%    #we only want logs from mobile loggers which are from 1001
            mutate(Date=ymd_hms(as.POSIXct(Date,format="%H:%M:%S %d/%m/%Y")))

#merge data with distance and date
logs.dist<-logs %>% 
       #    left_join(Position,by="ID") %>%   
           arrange(Date) %>% 
        #   distinct(ID,Date,.keep_all= TRUE) %>%  #no need to actually get rid of duplicates
      #     mutate(Interval=cut(Date,breaks=c(dist$Time.start[1],dist$Time.end),
      #                         labels=F)) %>%   #assign distance with logs and time
           # left_join(dist, by="Interval",all.y=F) %>% 
           mutate(ID=as.factor(ID),
                  Test=Test)

return(logs.dist)
}

#xlfile<-here("Data","2022-04-01_test_mobile_loggers","2022_03_14_test_distance_time.xlsx")
date.exp<-ymd("2022-03-14")
data.path<-here("Data","2022-04-01_test_mobile_loggers")
test<-1

# position<-data.frame(ID=c(13,11,2,17,5,10),
#                      Height=c(1.7,1.7,1.25,1.25,0.6,0.6),
#                      Position=1)

Logs.test1<-get_formatted_data(#XLfile=xlfile,  #excel file linking distance and time intervals
                             Date=date.exp,    #date of experiment
                             Path.Data=data.path,  #path to the folder containing log data from stationary loggers
                           #  Position=position,
                             Test=test)
```

# Figures for stationary loggers according to tests

```{r common figures,echo=F, fig.height=5,fig.width=15,message=F}

#bind data from different tests
aft<-ymd_hms(as.POSIXct("2022-04-01 13:30:00",format="%F %H:%M:%S"))

logs.all<-Logs.test1 %>% 
  filter(Date > aft) %>% 
   mutate(ID=as.factor(ID),
          Mobile=as.factor(Mobile))

#count nb of logs
count<-logs.all %>% 
    group_by(ID,Test) %>% 
  mutate(#Height=paste("Height:", Height, "m"),
         Test=paste("Test:",Test)) %>% 
    count()

#plot nb of logs depending on stationary logger
gg<-ggplot(count, aes( y=n, x=as.factor(ID),fill=ID)) + 
    geom_bar(position="dodge", stat="identity") +
    labs(x="Logger ID",y="Logs") +
    facet_grid(Test~.) +
    ggtitle("Number of counts depending on height and position")
print(gg)

```

# Figures for mobile loggers according to tests

```{r mobile logger plots , echo=F, fig.height=5,fig.width=15}
count<-logs.all %>% 
    group_by(Mobile,ID,Test) %>% 
    mutate(Mobile=as.factor(Mobile),
           ID=as.factor(ID),
           Test=Test) %>% 
    count()

#plot nb of logs depending on stationary logger and test number
gg<-ggplot(count, aes(fill=Mobile, y=n, x=ID)) + 
    geom_bar(position="dodge", stat="identity") +
    labs(x="Stationary logger ID",y="Logs") +
    facet_grid(Test~Mobile) +
    ggtitle("Number of counts depending on stationary logger")
print(gg)

```
